_note: Per-unit prices in USD. Override these with your tenant-specific contracted rates. Last reviewed against public price lists 2026-05-11; verify against your enterprise agreement before quoting.

cu._note: Azure AI Content Understanding GA (2025-11-01). Source: https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/content-understanding/ and https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/ai-services/content-understanding/pricing-explainer. Total cost = Content Extraction + Contextualization tokens + LLM input/output tokens + Embeddings tokens. CU charges PER 1,000 PAGES (or per hour for audio/video). The tier (minimal/basic/standard) is chosen by what processing CU actually performs: digital files (DOCX, XLSX, HTML, TXT, etc.) -> minimal; image-based with OCR-only (Read) -> basic; image-based with layout analysis -> standard. Contextualization tokens (`usage.contextualizationToken`) accrue whenever generative features are used (field extraction, figure analysis, segmentation, categorization, training). LLM tokens (`usage.tokens`) are billed by the underlying Foundry model deployment.

di._note: Azure AI Document Intelligence pricing. Source: https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/ai-document-intelligence/ (S0 Pay-As-You-Go, < 1M pages/mo). Rates are PER 1,000 PAGES. Commitment tiers and Read's high-volume break ($0.60/1k above 1M pages) are not modeled — adjust per_1000_pages.* if you have a contracted rate.

openai._note: Per-1K-token rates. Used when CU's response includes a `usage.tokens` block keyed by model name. Add new model rows as CU starts using new models. Keys match CU's token keys WITHOUT the `-input`/`-output` suffix.

compute._note: Local pipeline compute (LibreOffice render, TIFF rasterization). Modeled as a flat per-run cost amortized over typical workloads. Set to 0 to exclude.
